sections.about: This metric measures water consumption per rupee of revenue from operations under BRSR Principle 6 Essential Indicator water intensity per rupee of turnover. It is useful for capital-market comparability but can be distorted by steel price cycles, product mix, alloy/stainless value addition and revenue recognition. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Jindal leads in all three years, at 0.03587 L/₹ in FY2025. JSW ranks 2nd at 0.0424 L/₹. Tata worsens from 0.063 in FY2024 to 0.074 in FY2025, but ranks ahead of SAIL, which deteriorates to 0.089. Jindal’s BRSR reports water intensity per turnover as 358.67 KL/Crore INR for FY2025, equivalent to the standardised 0.03587 L/₹, and notes PPP-adjusted disclosure based on IMF conversion factors.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata ranks 3rd. Jindal is best at 0.03587 L/₹, making Tata’s 0.074 L/₹ approximately 106% higher. Tata moves from 4th in FY2024 to 3rd in FY2025 because SAIL deteriorates more sharply, but Tata’s own performance worsens.
sections.targets: No turnover-intensity-specific targets identified. Tata’s water reduction and recycling framework is relevant but operational rather than revenue-normalised.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Revenue-normalised intensity is sensitive to steel prices, product mix, stainless versus carbon steel value, and rupee turnover effects. Physical-output intensity is more reliable for operational benchmarking.
